channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: ARC Raiders Third Expedition Project Guide—Exact Expedition Window & Project Requirements
item.content:encoded:
Even though the second Expedition Window has slammed shut in ARC Raiders, the third Expedition Window is already looming overhead. The Rust Belt may be filled with freshly wiped accounts, but it’s never too early to start looking ahead towards the next wipe.

Of course, you don’t have to reset, but Embark Studios is actively working to improve the rewards and requirements in an effort to make the decision more appealing. By the time the third Expedition Window opens, it might be a no-brainer to wipe your account, so you’ll want to make sure you’re prepared.

Completing the Project is no quick and easy feat, so it’s best to get a head start. With that in mind, here’s when the third Expedition Window will open in ARC Raiders, and what you’ll need to complete the Project.

When Is ARC Raiders’ Third Expedition Window?

The third Expedition Window will open 58 days after the second one closed, so on April 29, 2026, meaning you’ve got until the end of April to complete the Expedition Project.

That’s almost two months from when the Project first becomes available, and if you didn’t complete the previous Expedition, you’ll still have whatever progress you made towards it saved from before. You can find the live countdown when you preview the Project in-game, so you can always stay up to date.

  • More Insider Gaming: Hurricane Map Condition, Explained

Once the Expedition Window has opened, raiders typically have around four to five days to confirm their departure. At this moment in time, Embark Studios hasn’t confirmed the dates yet, but assuming it sticks to the same schedule, expect the wipe to happen around May 3, 2026.

It might seem like that’s a lot of time to complete the Expedition Project, but be warned, you’ll need a lot of different resources if you want to finish it in time. It’s best to start sooner rather than later, as you might wind up missing the wipe and regretting it.

What Are The Requirements For The Third Expedition Project In ARC Raiders?

There are once again six stages to the Expedition Project in ARC Raiders, with each one having four requirements.

  • More Insider Gaming: ARC Raiders Map Condition Schedule

The items vary from materials to ARC parts, but the toughest stage is bound to be the fifth one, which requires you to sell 750,000 Coins’ worth of supplies! The Expedition won’t come cheap, but if you want to start completing it, here are all of the Project’s stages:

Will you be embarking on ARC Raiders’ next Expedition? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more ARC Raiders content, check out how to solve the Controlled Access Zone Button Puzzle on Dam Battlegrounds, as well as how to get Comet Igniters.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Heinz Ketchup Teases Surprise Pokémon Collab
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon fever is once again spreading across the globe, celebrating the franchise’s 30th anniversary, and Heinz has teased a bizarre collab.

Pokémon Day took place on Friday, February 27, showcasing a reveal trailer for the next generation, Pokémon Winds and Waves, alongside a heap of other news, and it seems The Pokémon Company has even more up its sleeves.

Pokémon is Getting Heinz Ketchup Collab

Heinz’s Japanese social media account posted a tease about a surprise collab with Pokémon on the agenda, telling fans to “look forward to the announcement coming soon.”

Pokémon is no stranger to huge collabs, as they recently partnered with LEGO to release the first Pokémon sets, and Japan has always created a frenzy for Pokémon crossover content, with everything from huge billboards to train makeovers advertising Pokopia, which releases this week.

Many may not immediately see Heinz as the most obvious of collab partners for Pokémon, however, particularly in Japan, where they instead name Ash Ketchum Satoshi, after Pokémon creator Satoshi Tajiri.

This means that, unless there is a global collab, Heinz can’t utilize phrases like ‘Gotta Ketch ‘Em All’ or release ‘Ash Ketchup,” so it’s hard to predict what Pokémon plans with the company.

More details should be revealed soon, however, and either way, the Pokémon collab with Heinz should lead to some saucy content.

What collabs would you like to see for Pokémon in the 30th anniversary year? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon, find out what region Pokémon Winds and Waves is based on, and learn how to get Nidoran in Fire Red and Leaf Green.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Ex-Highguard Dev Says Wildlight Approach Matched His ‘Biggest Fears’
item.content:encoded:
A former Highguard developer says Wildlight’s approach to competitive play leaned into his “biggest fears,” ultimately contributing to the game’s downfall.

Highguard has endured a significant fall from grace since its release on January 26, with player numbers plummeting and Tencent pulling funding, and it seems some devs had concerns before launch.

Highguard’s Competitive Focus Matched Dev’s ‘Biggest Fears’

Speaking on the Quad Damage Podcast, as transcribed by PCGN, former Wildlight level designer Alex Graner revealed the concerns he had about the approach of the game.

“I can only speak to my side of it as a level designer, but when I joined [Wildlight], it was trying to figure out this new, ambitious game, and this team is always pushing the boundaries.

“You don’t strive to create something that doesn’t work out, but it happens, unfortunately. Throughout development, we really leaned into the competitive side of it, and that was always one of my biggest fears as a player.

“3v3 duos is always the sweatiest version of anything like battle royale, objective modes, wingman, you know it, you name it. It requires such a high intensity of communication with your team, and team play, that it doesn’t leave much room for casualness. I think that was the biggest thing that turned a lot of players off Highguard.”

That approach may have alienated solo players who didn’t have teammates to squad up with, or new players jumping in for the first time, and Graner believes the approach works “at a really high level,” it’s “a lot to grasp at first.”

Additionally, Graner explained the 3v3 format requires higher skill, resulting in a high bar of entry, and that, if you don’t work together with teammates, you’re “going to get rolled” as “it’s very hard to 1v2 in our game.”

Since its launch, Highguard developer Wildlight has seen mass layoffs take place, with the remaining developers trying to claw back life into the title.

So far, though, there’s little sign of a resurgence, with the weekly Steam player peak below 1,000 since February 23, and the numbers continue to drop.

What do you think are the reasons for Highguard’s failure? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more news, take a look at the ‘next Palworld’ Neoran Valley smashing its fundraising target, and read about a potential Game of War spin-off title.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: NYT Mini Crossword Hints Today—Answers For Monday, March 2
item.content:encoded:
The NYT Mini Crossword is a popular, quick-to-complete word game to test yourself daily, but some of the puzzles can be difficult to complete, so here are some hints.

If you’ve found yourself stuck on today’s NYT Mini, Insider Gaming has some helpful hints and all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on Monday, March 2, 2026.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of the NYT Mini Crossword. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How to Play NYT Mini Crossword

The NYT Mini Crossword is available to play on the New York Times Games app or website, but, as of September 2025, a New York Times Game subscription is required.

The NYT Games Subscription is priced at $6.95 per month, but there are introductory offers available for other NYT subscriptions that include games access, which might be worth considering.

Without a Games subscription, access to the NYT Mini Crossword is blocked.

NYT Mini Crossword Clues (March 2)

Here are all the clues provided for the NYT Mini Crossword for March 2, 2026.

Across

1 – “Yeah, why not!”

5 – Collector’s ___

6 – Worker who frequently takes off

8 – Stressful way to run

9 – Crossed (out)

Down

1 – Tiny drink

2 – Useful… or a useful draw in Scrabble when you have a “Q”

3 – Chill

4 – Be expressive with one’s feelings

7 – TV’s ___ Lasso

Additional Clues For NYT Mini (March 2)

Here are some additional clues to help you solve the NYT Mini Crossword on March 2, 2026.

Across

1 – Certain of something

5 – A useful ___, like in a video game

6 – Debut episode of a new TV show

8 – “Don’t be ___!”

9 – Struck through

Down

1 – “I’ll have a small ___”

2 – Advantageous

3 – Frankie Goes To Hollywood hit

4 – Like a dance in Fortnite

7 – Title of 2021 filming starring Mark Wahlberg

All NYT Mini Crossword Answers (March 2)

Here are all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on March 2, 2026.

Across

1 – SURE

5 – ITEM

6 – PILOT

8 – LATE

9 – EXED

Down

1 – SIP

2 – UTILE

3 – RELAX

4 – EMOTE

7 – TED

When is The Next Game?

The NYT Mini Crossword refreshes daily at 10 PM ET on weekdays and Saturdays, and at 6 PM ET on Sundays.

As such, the next NYT Mini Crossword will be available at 7 PM PT/9 PM CT/10 PM ET/3 AM GMT/2 PM AET.

What is The NYT Mini Crossword?

The NYT Mini Crossword was introduced in 2014 as a shorter and quicker-to-complete game to accompany the main New York Times Crossword, which was initially released in 1942.

Until September 2025, the NYT Mini Crossword was free, but it has now been consolidated into a monthly NYT Games Subscription alongside several other popular games.

Did you struggle with the NYT Mini Crossword today? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more word games, take a look at hints and the answer for today’s Contexto, and check out the answers for today’s Connections.

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Today’s Contexto #1261: Hints & Answer For March 2, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, March 2, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Movable cover
  • Hint 2: Top of a container
  • Hint 3: Put a _ on it

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Glass
  • Plate
  • Put
  • Bottom
  • Bottle
  • Plastic
  • Seal
  • Top
  • Cap

Today’s Contexto Answer For Game #1261 On March 2, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Lid

A Lid is a movable cover for the opening of a container.

When Is The Next Contexto Game After #1261?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on March 3

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Check out some of Insider Gaming’s other word-game guides down below:

  • Wordle
  • NYT Connections

MOBILE APP
